stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 3 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 10 observations with mean 35.1 % (within-factory SD = 8.9). factory B has 5 observations with mean 60.7 % (within-factory SD = 3.2). factory C has 5 observations with mean 55.6 % (within-factory SD = 4.4). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.8 and 14.4 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory A: 36.0 % (shrunk from raw 35.1). Range [35.5, 37.3] as between-factory SD varies from 5.8 to 14.4. Pooled mean: 46.6.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 46.6 % (weighted average of 3 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=9.6: B = 0.921.
Interval [35.5, 37.3] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (35.1) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (46.6) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
